title: Dolicholus calycosus
intro: Dolicholus calycosus, also known as Hound's-tongue, is a perennial herbaceous plant belonging to the Boraginaceae family, native to Europe, Asia, and North Africa. The plant has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial effects, making it beneficial for treating various health issues such as wounds, skin infections, and respiratory problems. The bioactive constituents of Dolicholus calycosus include alkaloids, flavonoids, and terpenoids, which are responsible for its therapeutic effects. In traditional herbal medicine, Dolicholus calycosus is often used to make teas, infusions, and tinctures, which are commonly used to treat colds, coughs, and fever, as well as to promote digestive health.
taxonomy: Dolicholus calycosus is a flowering plant in the family Fabaceae. It is native to tropical Africa. The plant is classified within the subfamily Faboideae. Dolicholus calycosus is further classified into the tribe Phaseoleae. Within the tribe, it is placed within the genus Dolicholus.
